meaning: adj.未修订的,未订正的,未校订的,未修改的
examples: GDP in the euro area fell by 0.2% in the second quarter, unrevised from a provisional estimate, leaving it 1.4% higher than a year earlier.
In the unrevised model of the universe these two breakdown pathways ought to occur equally often.
Ladies-in-waiting and courtiers sought it out, even stealing unrevised pages from her room.
The figures show an unrevised rise of 0.9% on the quarter and year-on-year growth of 1.8%, the Office for National Statistics said.
